description:
You are working as a Linux systems-engineer who maintains the hardening scripts stored in
    /home/user/sys-hardening/.

    Current situation (already present on disk):
      1. /home/user/sys-hardening/VERSION  
         – Contains the single line: 2.1.9
      2. /home/user/sys-hardening/CHANGELOG.md  
         – Contains exactly the 10 lines shown below:

            # Change Log
            All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

            ## [Unreleased]
            ### Added
            - Implemented new SSL cipher suites.
            - Added automated backup rotation.

            ### Fixed
            - Corrected typo in Nginx worker_connections directive.

    Task you must accomplish:
      A. Perform a semantic version “minor” bump from 2.1.9 ➜ 2.2.0.  
         • Update /home/user/sys-hardening/VERSION so its single line is now: 2.2.0

      B. Move the whole “Unreleased” section in CHANGELOG.md to a new version entry
         headed by “## [2.2.0] - 2024-01-01”.  
         • The heading line must be exactly two hash symbols, a space, “[2.2.0]”, a space,
           a dash, another space, and the date string “2024-01-01”.  
         • All of the existing “### Added / ### Fixed …” bullets must remain under this new
           header, unchanged and in the same order.  
         • Immediately above the new header, insert a fresh empty
           “## [Unreleased]” section (only that single header line, no blank lines or
           bullets beneath it).

         The resulting top of CHANGELOG.md (first 14 lines) must therefore be **exactly**
         the following:

            # Change Log
            All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

            ## [Unreleased]

            ## [2.2.0] - 2024-01-01
            ### Added
            - Implemented new SSL cipher suites.
            - Added automated backup rotation.

            ### Fixed
            - Corrected typo in Nginx worker_connections directive.

      C. Create a plain-text log file named /home/user/version_bump.log
         containing exactly the single line:
            Bumped to 2.2.0

    Constraints & notes:
      • You may use no more than 3 shell commands to complete everything.  
      • Do not hard-code any absolute paths except those given above.  
      • No sudo or root privileges are available.  
      • The automated test will strictly compare the VERSION file, the first 14 lines
        of CHANGELOG.md, and the entire contents of version_bump.log against the
        specifications above.
truth:
Initial files (before the agent starts):
      /home/user/sys-hardening/VERSION
        2.1.9\n

      /home/user/sys-hardening/CHANGELOG.md
        # Change Log\n
        All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.\n
        \n
        ## [Unreleased]\n
        ### Added\n
        - Implemented new SSL cipher suites.\n
        - Added automated backup rotation.\n
        \n
        ### Fixed\n
        - Corrected typo in Nginx worker_connections directive.\n

    Expected final files after successful task execution:

      /home/user/sys-hardening/VERSION
        2.2.0\n

      /home/user/sys-hardening/CHANGELOG.md
        # Change Log\n
        All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.\n
        \n
        ## [Unreleased]\n
        \n
        ## [2.2.0] - 2024-01-01\n
        ### Added\n
        - Implemented new SSL cipher suites.\n
        - Added automated backup rotation.\n
        \n
        ### Fixed\n
        - Corrected typo in Nginx worker_connections directive.\n

      /home/user/version_bump.log
        Bumped to 2.2.0\n
